text: If you've ever spent days in Excel before Marit Week or anytime leadership asks,
who's underpaid, you know the fear. You might still miss something. I built shift
works HR for that moment. Upload your compensation census and an under 30
seconds you get who needs attention what it's going to cost to fix and the
summary leadership can actually use. You already have the file Workday, ADP,
UKG or a spreadsheet. Drop it in, no new HRS, nothing for IT to configure.
First, a prioritize list of what's going to be fixed before letters go out,
not 50 tabs. Critical items rise to the top so you know where to start. Then the
number of finances going to ask for cost to bring people up to the range, what's in
the Miracle and your total exposure. So you're not guessing in the meeting. Every
flag is a real person, name, department and the exact dollar gap. Act on it the
same day instead of rebuilding another spreadsheet. It also catches the
awkward ones, a manager earning less than someone they lead so you can handle
it before it becomes a surprise. When leadership asks about pay fairness you can
show median pay by gender and race from your own data ready for the
conversation. Need to brief executives, one click, risk, exposure and the
talking points that they're going to ask about. Try shift works HR free with
your own census, no credit card required.
